result: If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—the false belief that we are isolated selves, fundamentally divided from each other, from nature, and from the deeper fabric of existence. This single shift would unravel at the root the fear, greed, and cruelty that arise from seeing the “other” as a threat or a resource. From that dissolution would flow a natural empathy, a sense of shared being, and a spontaneous reverence for life that no law or moral code could ever enforce. It’s the one change that doesn’t just rearrange the pieces but transforms the game entirely.
